byKATU Staff

CANNON BEACH, Ore. (KATU) —It’s a rare sighting along the Oregon Coast, two healthy sea otters have been spotted at Ecola Point near Cannon Beach, according to theElakha Alliance.

They say sea otters have been absent from the Oregon Coast since the early 1900s when they were hunted to local extinction for their fur.

The alliance believes the pair of otters swam down from the Olympic Coast of Washington.

Sea otters are not normally a migratory species, which is why a local population has not reestablished itself.

The alliance says the sighting is a hopeful sign.



2025Sinclair, Inc.
title: Sea otters spotted at Cannon Beach, a hopeful sign after a century absence
description: It’s a rare sighting along the Oregon Coast, two healthy sea otters have been spotted at Ecola Point near Cannon Beach, according to the Elakha Alliance.
